---
title: "Quiver"
---

Advanced mathematics for cybersecurity, presented as validated Haskell and Python lessons.

## Lesson Categories

- [Foundations](lessons/foundations/index.qmd) — Linear algebra, calculus, discrete math, probability
- [Network Theory](lessons/network-theory/index.qmd) — Graph theory, flow networks, routing algorithms
- [Complexity Theory](lessons/complexity-theory/index.qmd) — Computability, P vs NP, cryptographic hardness
- [Machine Learning](lessons/ml/index.qmd) — Statistical learning, neural networks, optimization

## How Lessons Work

Each lesson ships as:

- A **Haskell** file runnable via `runghc lesson.hs`
- A **Python** file runnable via `uv run python lesson.py`
- A **Quarto** page (this wiki) importing the LaTeX-rendered versions of both

To scaffold a new lesson:

```bash
just new-lesson <topic> <name>
just validate lessons/<topic>/<name>/lesson.hs
just export-latex lessons/<topic>/<name>
```
